standard_refs.title: RFC 7252 - The Constrained Application Protocol (CoAP)
standard_refs.notes: Core RFC for message format, Token, Message ID, Options, and matching.

standard_refs.title: RFC 8323 - CoAP over TCP, TLS, and WebSockets

reason_for_selection: Best first RFC-based pilot because it is compact but semantically rich.
Token, Message ID, Options, Observe, and Block-wise transfer naturally expose implicit state and parser constraints.
libcoap, FreeCoAP, microcoap, and go-coap allow cross-implementation comparison.

potential_implicit_constraint_points.description: Token must preserve request-response association and must not be truncated or reused across contexts.
Malformed option length/delta encodings must not be reinterpreted as other fields.
Proxy hostnames derived from request options need explicit bounds before address resolution.

notes: CoAP should become the first strictly RFC-based pilot if MQTT is considered only a motivation case.
